validation gate: hard.next_step 'Working on the bid with EY - They are sending over the document today - We've completed an initial assessment of the GCA requirement, and the picture is very positive — we're scoring this as a strong pursue currently. We're just working through a small number of internal validations with our solutioning and infrastructure teams around a few risk items, but nothing that gives us cause for concern at this stage.Next step is to actively follow up with Crown Commercial Service after the completed tender submission to confirm the formal evaluation process, key milestones, decision timeline, and next actions required from Zycus. The opportunity has progressed to Formal Evaluation with a value of $750,000 and a close date of 7 May 2027, but qualification gaps remain around Budget and Authority. The immediate focus should be to identify the economic buyer, decision maker, procurement evaluator, technical evaluator, and business champion within CCS, as the current opportunity record does not yet show a mapped decision-making structure. Zycus should engage existing contacts and request clarity on who will be involved in evaluating the S2P solution, what criteria will be used, how budget approval will be handled, and whether there are upcoming clarification sessions, demos, commercial reviews, or compliance checkpoints. The account team should also build a multi-threaded engagement plan across procurement, finance, digital transformation, compliance, and supplier management stakeholders to reduce single-threading risk and improve deal control. Internally, Zycus should prepare a tailored value case focused on CCS’s need to improve public sector procurement efficiency, strengthen compliance, increase transparency, reduce supplier management complexity, and support AI-led digital transformation. Success in the next stage depends on confirming the buying committee, validating budget ownership, understanding the evaluation process, and positioning Zycus as the best-fit AI-powered S2P platform for CCS’s public sector procurement modernization goals.' diverges from Salesforce '

Confidence 10%

5th May - High-level quick demo provided on their priority areas iSupplier/iSource (touched on Intake& ANA), received well (only gap I can see at this point is that they want to advertise their opportunities to the market, as Jaggaer does with public sector clients) - next step is to brief HOP and agree a way forward, have offered to provide a summary/pitch/demo to HOP - ball is in thier court, chase if nothing heard in a week or two.

29th April - Positive discovery call (SIR below), Next Steps: a) A live demo focussing on a high-level overview of the full Source-to-Pay suite (though initial focus is likely to be Sourcing & Supplier Management, with perhaps Intake Management to step change the user community experience, with CLM & Performance/Risk Management as a later phase.).

b) Option to support the business case/ROI if required? - we will discuss after the demo session.

SITUATION

•Careem is newly rolling out Oracle eSourcing; adoption is low and they are simultaneously trying to digitize historically manual/email‑based quote collections and sourcing.

•The Oracle interface is too complex for non‑tech sourcing managers and for suppliers, so they are exploring other options.

•The project is owned by the controllership/procurement excellence team, who are also pushing broader automation and agentic AI across Oracle processes.

IMPACT

•There is “double pressure”: they must both digitize manual processes and make the new digital tool actually usable, otherwise adoption and digitization targets will fail.

•Supplier onboarding and training on complex tools is viewed as the biggest challenge; if the UI isn’t intuitive, supplier participation in eSourcing events will suffer.

•Usability and adoption of the live Oracle tool are already “challenged,” and they have a hard deadline not to exit 2026 without full digitization in this area.

PROPOSED RESOLUTION

•Zycus is a UX‑friendly, AI‑driven S2P layer that complements Oracle: with much easier eSourcing, Supplier Management/Onboarding, and guided Intake - while Oracle stays the ERP system of record.\xa0

•Current thinking: start with an eSourcing & Autonomous Negotiation‑focused deployment, plus likely supplier management and Intake for step changing user experience/interface. Then phase in CLM and supplier risk/performance in later phases, aligned to their budget and digitization roadmap.

22nd May - MSA and Order 1 close to completion with Mair legal- will have full update by Monday 25th\xa0

May 19th - Redlines on MSA received today, (thanks to Zoheb for a quick turnaround) - some back and forth on clauses and a negotiation needed on a termination for convenience - reverted.

May 18th - Following emergency below, Mair head of legal has agreed to continue reviewing our paper, feedback/redlines expected Tues 19th or Weds 20th (Zoheb continues to review theirs in parallel).

May 14th - Head of legal working on our paper has had to leave the country for a family emergency, unclear on his availability to resolve currently - Zoheb is working on their template as a fallback and will update me on 19th May.

May 13th - Lovemer has a meeting on 14th with Omar (Mair Head of Legal) to discuss the MSA/OF will update us asap afterwards with feedback.

May 12th - Reminded them for the SOW workshop - Hrishi +1 (& Dan) will travel and hold onsite to minimise any risk, date TBC.

May 11th - Mair legal are reviewing Zycus MSA and hopeful to agree on using our paper- will know in next 2 days (this will greatly accelerate signing)

No immediate blockers in Ts & Cs.

May 8th - LOI received - likely MSA/Order 1 sign off "before Eid break" (i.e. before 26th May) - pushing for sooner.

May 7th - All issues resolved, some back & forth on implementation phasing, but nothing to worry about, verbal comment from Head of Proc Ops: "Everything appears to be ok for approval today/tomorrow & LOI early next week".

May 6th - Meeting below held: (positive session), mostly based on clarifications of: a) implementation invoicing across phases (resolved), b) number of onsite teams (resolved), c) travel budget (Outstanding - they are asking us to swallow it all - we are pushing back, to split 50/50 as this stage - action for them to confirm, otherwise potentially intro Carl to Mazin-CPO), d) we also raised the legal paperwork, their policy/preference is their own standard template, (Outstanding - not a showstopper, but made a case that our paper is Saas/Zycus fit for purpose and faster/easier to edit than rewriting theirs, action for them to confirm). Intention is to resolve these issues by the end of the week, ideally then to submit LOI by EOW (Fri 8th May) and signed Contracts before Eid public holiday (w/c Mon 25th May).

May 4th - Provided some clarifications (Implementation onsite support, payment terms, travel) - they followed up with a request for a call on Weds 6th to discuss: Phased Implementation plan timeline, Payment Terms and Travel fees - Carl, Tanya & Hrishi are joining, (also meeting on 5th to strategize).

30th April/May 1st - Some back & forth clarifications, nothing concerning.

29th April - Comment from CPO after a nudge: "Still going through some internal review process. Hope to close this asap".

27th April - Requested (already sent) and resent MSA & Oder Form templates for internal review, also completed a vendor registration form and provided documentation (Trade Licence, TRN, Bank Details etc.), made clear that using our paper is preferred but not a showstopper.

24th April - They acknowledged receipt of BAFO, are reviewing will revert next week, looking like they will push for their paper, asked to discuss with CPO as ours is Saas/Zycus fit for purpose, faster, mutual, no risk etc. etc.

23rd April - Liaised with Carl offered some BAFO concessions (through discount options held over for this phase e.g. Publicity 5%), license pricing mostly protected in return for: a) min 5 years, b) strong reference-ability/PR and c) speed of decision (end May signature latest) - BAFO & MSA/Oder Form templates shared - Next Step: requested a call with CPO Fri/Sat/Sun to discuss.

21st April - F2F meeting went well, asking us to sweeten the deal in year 1, but in return have agreed to 5 years (started the meeting committing to only 3), will be a reference customer and a quick decision - followed up with an mail confirming & asking for closing BAFO price with preference to Zycus- some niggly terms we should be able to navigate, due Thursday, with formal decision expected by mid-May, liaising with Carl to review.

16th April - Next step is a face-to-face meeting in Abu Dhabi to discuss commercials & implementation, Dan & Tanya will be on-site with Hrishi dialling in - will synch with Carl prior to discuss commercial tactics.

9th April - Clarifications and revised implementation pricing (now a longer phased approach) hand delivered - retained original SaaS pricing in expectation of a negotiation (champion tip), made clear the assumption is a single phased contract, digital copies also added to their Showpad link. NOTE: Some issues where we were not able to meet their requirements/match GEP i.e. RTO/RPO (concerns due to current war situation and AWS taken out in Dubai & Bahrain) - champion thinks it won't be a showstopper.

6th April - Revised phasing and further formal clarifications received - response to be hard copy/hand delivered to Abu Dhabi office Thurs latest.

3rd April - Positive clarifications call, some push to improve RTO/RPO and uptime to match the competition, but doesn't appear to be a showstopper, also looking to move away from big bang to a phased approach focussing on priority modules e.g. Sourcing first, not clear economic impact yet, but have indicated it will still be a single contract - follow up call with the champion afterwards confirmed informally we are the preferred vendor and are in competition with GEP, next steps they are going to confirm on 6th/7th what their phasing plan will be and we will need to review/update commercials accordingly - note GEP are 50% of our pricing in the Al Tasnim opp.

1st April - Clarification responses run-through call scheduled for Fri 3rd April.

30th Mar - Clarifications received and responses submitted.

25th March - Nudged Lovemer for an update on evaluation & timelines, Vishal (CTO) called later to clarify some RFP response elements, potentially a good sign as he said he is not supposed to do it and it's against the proc policies to give us an advantage, will clarify and respond asap today/tomorrow.

6th March - RFP Technical & Commercial response hand delivered to HQ in Abu Dhabi, response clarifications is next step. (Commercial positioned as a budgetary estimate as we had to guesstimate many data points, also held back on some discounts in anticipation of further negotiation).

26th Feb - Clarifications received (still many gaps, will need to guesstimate and make caveats in pricing) - deadline extended to 6th March - still expecting a hand delivered hard copy to Abu Dhabi.

10th Feb - RFP for S2P received - deadline is 25th Feb, clarifications 18th.... currently requires hard copy submission to Abu Dhabi, requested Showpad or alternative.

evidence_coverage.salesforce_window: Q1-Q3 SOQL reads (standard mechanics, authoritative direct fields, synthesis-source fields); OpportunityFieldHistory trailing 365d; Tasks and Events 90d; Contact Roles; OpportunityLineItem

gaps: Zero Avoma calls discovered via opp+account+attendee-email method. All deal intelligence reconstructed from Salesforce Next_Step__c / Next_Step_History__c, which is a detailed rep-maintained log. This is a partner-led / APAC deal per ground-truth note; partner likely runs the calls and does not record them in Avoma. However, the absence of buyer voice (no verbatim quotes, no named speakers, no call recordings) means competitive dynamics, MEDDPICC, and buyer sentiment are inferred from rep notes rather than directly observed. This lowers confidence in the competitive read (Ariba status post-Dec 2025 unknown) and MEDDPICC depth (EB unmapped, champion strength uncertain).

Ariba status post-December 2025 contract expiration is unknown. Original forcing event was Ariba contract ending Dec 2025 per 14 Oct 2025 disco, but that deadline passed without a Zycus close and no record exists of what happened (Ariba renewed? extended? displaced by another vendor? running without contract?). This is a genuine knowledge gap that affects the competitive read and urgency assessment.

Economic Buyer is unmapped after 246 days. Omar KAAKI (Director Procurement) is Decision Maker but not C-level, and he is building a budgetary business case internally to present up for approval per 11 May and 15 June, but the CFO/CEO/sponsor who will approve is not identified. This is a genuine knowledge gap that blocks close-plan visibility and increases vulnerability to competitor executive escalation.

Multi-threading is shallow: only Omar consistently engaged; Nada Hallal introduced 28 Apr but no follow-up call recorded; Naveen Pinto (Influencer per Contact Role) not mentioned in any Next Step activity. This limits visibility into broader stakeholder sentiment and buying-committee dynamics.

18th of June 2026 - intro request shared with the prospect to schedule for a meeting Next week to evaluate our position .
Confidence 30%
15th June - Spoke to Omar, the war has caused some delays on the project, however it is still continuing but now looking like a Q4 deal for implementation to start Q1 - will get the usage numbers across to us by end June, for budgetary estimate, then expecting an RFP to be launched in October.

11th May - Spoke to Omar, they are working out all the usage numbers across the various business units, looking to get the answers back for a QIT/Budgetary estimate ideally by end of this week, offered help, but are building it out internally first.

28th April - Some positive signals: Omar enquired how to get a proposal, shared estimation tables and requested a call to walk through with him this week, he also introduced us to the IT lead, shared the security datasheet as a courtesy asked for a call this week how we can support her.

26th April - Received some clarifications due Weds 29th April, provided Mon 27th.

23rd April - Demo session with team went well - next steps TBC next week: likely to want to drill into more detail on functionality, (savings, specific workflows) structuring multi-business units, infosec, SAP ECC integration - offered onsite workshops if travel is an option with the current conflict.

6th April - CPO replied asking for a team demo after 13th April based on our BRD response and recommendations, date confirmed Thurs 23rd - 2 hour session.

9th Mar - RFI/BRD submitted, next step following review/shortlisting is demos/RFP aiming to be there F2F if travel is an option/Tanya can get a visa - dates not yet set.

4th Mar - RFI/BRD received (due 'next week') ahead of a formal RFP - scope as expected is S2P and looks like a good fit, will review in detail with Tanya asap.

16th Feb - CPO confirmed they will be initiating a formal evaluation, initial requirements will be shared end of Feb, have already shared some differentiators with him to be included.

26th Jan - Next step is a live demo, but having issues getting Tanya a Kuwait visa (takes 1 month and her application was cancelled for some reason after the first attempt) - now looking like early March - reached out to CPO to see if he prefers to wait or do it online with me onsite.

15th Jan - Met with Omar Kaaki Director of Group Procurement in Kuwait HQ, he apologised for the delay/lack of comms due to end of year focus on key priorities, looking to get things back on track and implement an AI powered S2P suite this year, likely by a formal RFP - A very positive meeting, and Zycus is resonating well with him. Strategy/next steps are

  • To try and avoid an RFP if possible, either way will influence the requirements documents in our favour by meeting for a f2f demo with the team in late Jan/early Feb - focussing hard on GenAI, Intake & ANA as differentiators, looking to build momentum before Ramadan.
  • Will aim to develop Omar into a Champion, will also look to build out/influence the business case in later stages (he has broadly agreed to this).
  • Challenges will be showing how we can adapt to multiple diverse business lines (automotive, manufacturing, retail, franchises etc.) - will keep this as a thread throughout.
  • If we can maintain this new pace & avoid an RFP it could be a Q1 deal, conservatively its Q2 for now, but will review as it evolves.

12th Dec - No response to multiple follow-ups, demo dates and invites, moving back to Initial Interest

22nd Oct - No response to demo dates shared - updated options and nudged (also offered f2f meeting in Kuwait ... can synch with Zain & NBK also)

15th Oct - NDA provided, signed and reverted

14th Oct - Positive Disco call held, unhappy Ariba customer looking to replace quickly decision likely end Dec, with some overlap with Ariba - Intro slides sent & NDA received - next steps to sign and NDA & arrange a demo with stakeholders (options shared)

\xa0

6/2/26 MQ Per Julio they are still reviewing submissions for down selection to shortlist.


4/28/26 MQ Per Julio, Antonino is traveling this week. Suggested reaching out 5/7 for update.


4/8/26 MQ RFP response submitted 3/31. Antonino and Julio are doing initial review of submitted proposals, specifically evaluating how each aligns with their medium-to-long term strategy. I am offering to provide business case to support their efforts. Antonino expects to have update on next steps the week of 4/20.


3/18/26 MQ Foundever has issued an RFP for CLM, Sourcing and Vendor Management and Onboarding. Responses are due by 3/31/26.

Foundever embarked in a Finance Transformation Project selecting SAP S4/Hana Public Cloud Edition for consolidating all ERP core Finance functionality across the Globe including the 42 Countries where Foundever has operations. The Implementation Project started January 2026 and will be executed in waves during next 2 years. First GoLive is January 2027 with 2 Countries and during 2027 and 2028 the remaining Countries to complete the 42 countries by the end of 2028.

As part of the scope, not all the P2P functionalities were considered and Foundever is exploring with potential recognized names on the P2P spectrum for completing those and selecting, ideally one partner, for covering all the missing functionalities. The intention is having an initial estimated cost of licensing and implementation by the end of March for presenting a Business Case to Executive Committee. Once approval obtained, refining proposals during April to June for awarding one or two Partners for covering the missing functionalities, starting the implementation during 2026 for GoLive January 2027 of the first 2 Countries.


3/5/26 MQ Foundever is standardizing/migrating all 42 sites to HANA/S4. Initial sites are US and Phillipines and are slated to go live 1/27. They will be using SAP for transactional P2P and have proposed to Coupa a 2yr. ramp down (Coupa is pushing back, renewal EOY). Looking to Zycus for CLM, eSourcing and Supplier Management with potential for additional footprint after Coupa ramp down. Wants us to go-live 1/27 on initial sites with other sites implemented concurrently w/ ERP roll out schedule. Want us to potentially replace existing Coupa P2P for 2 years if Coupa negotiations go south. Julio is sending 2yr. overall rollout scope.


3/2/26 MQ Scheduled meeting on 3/5/26 with Julio for update of project status.


2/19/26 MQ\xa0Working with Mariana and Julio to lock down date for group for Supplier Onboarding demonstration.

12/3/25 MQ Held scheduled process timing/alignment meeting with Julio. He wants preliminary cost estimates for S2P suite vs. Supplier Management (onboarding) and eSourcing modules. Julio will gather metrics for pricing. He is checking schedules to see if it's feasible to schedule the follow up Supplier Management demo in December or wait until January.

Time frame: week of June 8th\xa0or 15th.\xa0 I would anticipate that this will be an initial high-level discussion at first and create general concepts of total costs going forward.\xa0 More detailed review and contractual discussion when NU signs up for the program.

Second is a more hands on, in-depth, review of the tool set\xa0
  1. We will need to see it in action
    1. A site visit of one of your customers
    2. Live action with one student as buyer, two as sellers.
  2. Walk through the end-to-end process, in a live environment using NU spend data, and select product, put it out to bid. What We Want to See the AI Agent Do:
    1. Ingest context from a brief plain-language description of the need (typed or spoken) and generate a structured, category-appropriate RFP draft
    2. Automatically populate standard sections: scope of work, technical requirements, service levels, pricing model structure, evaluation criteria, and terms/conditions framework
    3. Reference historical category data and market benchmarks (e.g., Staffing Industry Analysts, Bureau of Labor Statistics rates) to inform rate card structure
    4. Suggest a recommended supplier shortlist based on category fit, past performance indicators, and diversity/ESG criteria
    5. Identify missing information and prompt the user to complete gaps — do not silently skip required fields
    6. Allow iterative refinement via conversational AI ("change the pricing structure to time-and-materials only" or "add a cybersecurity-specific SLA section")


The goal is to see if the following can be shown
DimensionWhat We Are Evaluating
1. Practical UtilityDoes the AI agent produce outputs that a seasoned procurement professional would actually use without significant rework? Is the quality of the RFP or negotiation strategy at a CPO-ready standard?
2. Reasoning TransparencyCan the evaluator understand why the agent made each decision? Is the AI's logic explainable, auditable, and defensible to a CFO or Board? Hidden black-box logic is disqualifying.
3. Human-AI TeamingIs the handoff between AI action and human decision well-designed? Does the system know what it doesn't know? Are escalation triggers meaningful and configurable?
4. Integration DepthDoes the AI agent leverage real organizational data (spend history, supplier master, contract terms) or operate in a generic vacuum? Can it connect to ERPs, market data feeds, or external databases?
5. Risk & Compliance DesignDoes the system enforce approval thresholds, flag non-compliant terms, maintain audit trails, and respect data privacy requirements? Is it enterprise-grade in its governance architecture?

from Salesforce '

Confidence 50%

3rd June - Spoke to Wheelson CEO, no update from Mumtalakat, gulf situation means project likely delayed by a few weeks, anticipating resumption/next steps ~late June.

15th April - Caught up with CEO of Wheelson for project update: Details are:

  • Confirmed the client is Mumtalaket - the Bahrani Sovereign Wealth Fund
  • Zycus has been shortlisted for phase 2 alongside Aerchain for the 'Enterprise' project track (and Zip for the 'Small' project track)
  • The war has caused some delay in the project, but expecting confirmation and clarification of next steps by mid May
  • Anticipated next step(s) will be a 1-2 day workshop in Bahrain demoing to stakeholders in the pilot organization
  • Initial plan was a paid proof of concept project with an enterprise pilot - prior to a roll-out across the group, not clear at this stage if such a roll-out will be mandated or optional - there are c60 entities of various sizes/shareholding - including: Gulf Air, Aluminum Bahrain, Batelco Group, McLaren Group, Nat Bank of Bahrain.
  • No immediate follow up actions until Mumtalakat/Wheelson confirm the way forward.

18th Mar - Final overview demo focussing on Sourcing, with Sourcing SMEs in attendance, next steps:

  • Wheelson to prepare a detailed SWAT-based comparative summary of all 5 vendors for the client’s two‑day workshop in the second week of April, aiming to narrow from 5 to 2 vendors.\xa0
  • Reach out to us over email with sharp / specific follow‑up questions or requests for deeper dives in particular areas, if needed before the April workshop.\xa0
  • If Zycus is shortlisted into the next stage, come back to us for support on a more detailed evaluation, including a potential pilot/POC for one entity.\xa0
  • Zycus provide additional clarifications, demos, or artefacts (e.g., screenshots like AI scoring, bid normalization details) whenever requested by Samynathan to support their internal analysis and April workshop.\xa0
  • Be on standby for a deeper workshop or paid POC if Zycus is selected into the final 2–3 vendors in the long‑term evaluation phase

16th Mar - Initial overview of Zycus I2O (excluding CLM - out of scope), plus live demo, sourcing drill-down to follow as next step - scheduled for 18th Mar

9th Mar - Intro overview call with CEO ..... client is confidential, but is a similar description to the Bahraini Sovereign Wealth fund: Mumtalakat Holding Company.

Situation

• Wheelson (consultancy / solutions integrator) is advising a Bahrain-based investment company (c. $20bn AUM, 60 entities) on selecting a comprehensive, end-to-end digital procurement platform.\xa0

• They currently use highly customized, point AI/GenAI solutions across multiple business areas (incl. procurement), which are hard to replicate or scale across customers.\xa0

• For this client, they started with point solutions for tender management, supplier intelligence, and P2P automation for some mid-scale entities; engagement has deepened to an overall consulting mandate.\xa0

Impact / Pain

• Existing work led to fragmented, non‑repeatable solutions and difficulty scaling products or reusing them across entities.\xa0

• Manufacturing/other sectors were slow to adopt AI; they’re looking for a domain where AI value is adopted more aggressively – hence pivot to procurement.\xa0

• The client has 12+ years of supplier data but essentially no external supplier intelligence and wants an “intelligent compilation” of internal + external data for supplier choice and relationship management.\xa0

• Procurement is treated as strategic, centrally governed from HQ across 60 entities, with hybrid central/decentral models, making governance, intake experience, and guided buying critical.\xa0

(Proposed) Resolution

• Wheelson is running a structured evaluation of “high edge” enterprise S2P suites; Zycus and Jaggaer are the final two contenders to complete initial evaluation.\xa0

• Plan is:



      • initial generic capabilities evaluation now (no NDA), then
      • for 2 shortlisted vendors a deeper NDA-based workshop
      • paid POC/POV for one entity, then scale to multiple entities if successful.\xa0

• Next step for Zycus specifically: show end-to-end “demand to PO” process (excluding CLM), strong intake & guided buying, supplier intelligence, sourcing, and ability to build/extend AI agents (Merlin, orchestration, etc.) without heavy OEM dependence.\xa0

\xa0

Confidence 10%

4th June - Currently evaluating expect feedback by end June, no clarifications yet.

22nd May - A light RFP submitted, a small deal, but they stress they have the budget, let's see.

14th May - After a couple of nudges, granted extension to SOW/RFP to Fri 22nd May, license usage for commercials requested.

1st May - Project delayed by the war, but now heading back on track, have shared an SOW for CLM & Sourcing - next steps: review and confirm/decline participation accordingly.

30th Jan - Originally an inbound Opp that we politely qualified out of as too small, they seemed to get a little irritated and have been quick to point out that they can get budget, and gave their Oracle ERP as an example - we shared the commercial estimation table to get an early estimate.

\xa0

  1. Send Detailed Proposal with Pricing Breakdown
    1. Deadline: By February 2026
    2. Details: Provide a detailed proposal with separate pricing for each module (CLM, RFQ, supplier risk, etc.). Include the integration details with Oracle and outline how Zycus can solve UHK’s procurement challenges. Highlight the economies of scale for purchasing multiple modules.
  2. Prepare Integration Documentation for Oracle
    1. Deadline: By February 2026
    2. Details: Provide Kerolos with technical documentation that explains how Zycus integrates with Oracle ERP, specifically how the solution will track contracts and link payment terms, ensuring seamless communication between the two systems.
  3. Confirm Demo Date for CLM and RFQ Modules
    1. Scheduled for: Week of January 26, 2026
    2. Goal: Deep dive into the contract management, RFQ process, and supplier risk management, showcasing how Zycus can optimize these processes and integrate smoothly with Oracle.
  4. Assist in Building Business Case
    1. Deadline: By End of February 2026
    2. Details: Provide Kerolos with a high-level summary of expected ROI, cost-saving opportunities, and efficiency gains that can be incorporated into the business case for management approval.
  5. Prepare for Follow-Up Call in March
    1. Timeline: March 2026
    2. Details: After UOWD’s management reviews the business case and approves the budget, plan a follow-up to discuss the next steps and finalize the tender process.

Need: Timothy expresses a clear need to understand how new tools, especially those leveraging AI, can benefit his role as a category manager and the procurement process within his organization. He highlights struggles with:

  • Poor end-to-end visibility across direct and indirect spend and contracts.
  • "Tail-spend leakage," poor catalog governance, and high off-contract purchases despite using Ariba P2P.
  • Supplier risk and compliance issues (environmental testing, sustainability).
  • Integration challenges with Ariba, especially with SAP S/4HANA migration halting previous progress.
  • Lack of leveraging Ariba for RFX space (using Jagger instead) and challenges with supplier risk and performance management (KPIs, analytics).
  • Using a separate system (Sirion) for contract management, leading to separate databases and poor user experience/dashboards.
  • Frustration with constant reinvention and lack of a uniform platform due to spinning off legacy SAP applications. He sees AI as a way to "catch up and also leverage it to be more efficient."

Timeline: Timothy estimates a formal project may be formed around 2026.\xa0

' (offending: 'Need: Timothy expresses a clear need to understand how new tools, especially those leveraging AI, can benefit his role as a category manager and the procurement process within his organization. July 20 - follow up


Procurement Strategy Update Meeting

Overview

Edward Dlugosz, George Andrus, and Lee Teney (the two US decision makers) discussed the status of their procurement initiative at Mizuho, which has been paused due to the need for a global procurement framework. Lee expressed gratitude for the team's work and acknowledged the six-month delay. Edward offered to provide information on Zycus' user group event in Colorado and mentioned a new client in the Middle East. George emphasized the importance of regular updates and mentioned Zycus' impressive technology and team. The team agreed to stay in touch and share updates as they become available.


Action Items:

  • Edward Dlugosz - Research Zycus’s penetration into the Japanese market and clients using the platform in native Japanese-based environments, and send a summary report to Lee, George, Matt, and Alex.
  • Edward Dlugosz - Send detailed information about the upcoming Zycus user group event (including dates, location, and agenda) to Lee and George today.
  • Edward Dlugosz - Provide periodic updates to Lee and George on company and product news, including new clients and deployments in Asia, as material developments occur.
  • Edward Dlugosz - Inform Lee and George of the name of the new Middle East client once the publicity clause allows public disclosure.
  • Edward Dlugosz - When significant material updates arise (such as new client wins or major product changes), ping Matt and Alex directly instead of waiting for the next monthly check-in.
  • Review the user group event information that Zycus sends and evaluate whether to attend.

Update on Mizuho Initiative

  • Lee Teney informs Edward Dlugosz and George Andrus that their management has asked for a longer pause on the Mizuho initiative.
  • The pause is due to the need for a global framework for procurement and cost management, which varies by region.
  • Lee Teney expresses gratitude for the work done so far and acknowledges the difficulty of the pause.
  • The pause is estimated to last at least six months, and the team is currently treating the project as on hold.
  • Edward Dlugosz confirms that Zycus has clients in Japan and shares an example of a long evaluation process with Panasonic North America.

Invitation to User Group Event

  • Edward Dlugosz invites the team to attend a user group event in Colorado, highlighting the benefits of meeting with analysts and other customers.
  • He offers to help with travel restrictions if necessary and promises to send information about the event.
  • The team expresses interest in the event and plans to review the information provided.
  • Edward Dlugosz suggests regular check-ins with Alex and Emmett to stay in touch and share updates.

Final Remarks and Next Steps

  • Edward Dlugosz asks if he should check in with Alex and Emmett on a monthly basis, and the team agrees.
  • Lee Teney and George Andrus discuss the importance of updates on company and product news, especially new clients in Asia.
  • Edward Dlugosz shares that Zycus has secured its first client in the Middle East, despite the challenges in the region.
  • The meeting concludes with the team expressing appreciation for the information and plans to stay in touch.

Need to get closer to the CPO (EB), Gaurav has potential as a champion, but we need to get more face time with both.

18th Sept - Caught up with Gaurav the project is still on, but has fallen behind schedule, partly the summer break and partly a change in approach: they have now enlisted Efficio (starting this week) to review their tech footprint and make recommendations (tech not vendor recommendation he stressed), they will produce a report/present to the board for sign-off by end 25, then commence a formal process in Jan/Feb 26 - promised to keep us up to date, nothing we can do now, he will share all our content with Efficio, I have asked if we can brief them and do a reminder session for Masdar, he said he will consider as it progresses. 23rd July - Still no pricing template - nudged SPOC Gaurav for a catch-up, confirmed he is on holiday until 12th August

7th July - Responded that he would be reviewing the business case asap and will forward the pricing template across likely this week.

NOTE: a delay in Oracle Fusion deployment means a likely delay in S2P, initially planning to start Dec 25/Jan 26 - now likley to be 2-3 months later ... will keep the close date as end of 24 for now, but review as we progress.

21st May - Value assessment underway call to review draft TBC on 26th/27th May (moved to 12th June)

12th May - Value assessment meeting scheduled to support building their business case (Shweta Pant assigned)

1st May - NDA signed on both sides, intro call to Values Assessment consultant being arranged.

07/04 - DQ demo was scheduled for 08/04, Guarav contacted to postpone, alternative options shared, Gaurav will revert within a day or two.

Situation

  • They use SAP Ariba for S2P, find it too complex and not user‑friendly for a “smaller nation”; integration with SAP is painful despite both being SAP products. They’re beginning an SAP ECC → S/4HANA migration.\xa0Ariba contract ends April 27 - intention is to have a replacement by end of 26.

Impact

  • Usability: They “don’t need that much complexity” but are stuck with it.\xa0
  • Integration: Ariba–SAP integration is “too complex” and they have to act as mediator between two SAP entities.\xa0

(Proposed) Resolution with Zycus

  • Replace Ariba with a simpler, user‑friendly, SAP‑integrated Zycus S2P platform covering procurement through to contracts, vendor, invoices, and shipments; start with a baseline phase (likely S2C+ Invoicing), then add advanced/AI capabilities on a roadmap/later phases.will push Intake considering their priority of a step change in UI/UX.

Situation

•EQUATE is a large Kuwaiti Petrochem co & uses SAP ERP (moving to S/4HANA in Feb) ,

•Has been on SAP Ariba for ~10 years, using only RFI/RFQ/RFP and reverse auctions,

•Most post‑bid work handled offline/manually,

•Exploring alternatives to Ariba after a positive Gartner mention of Zycus,

•Want to start with a small, budget‑sensitive scope focused on sourcing.\xa0

Impact

•Heavy offline workload: proposals downloaded, shared, evaluated, awarded and approved manually outside the system,

•Current eSourcing process is not fully digitalized,

•Low team adoption: 52 licensed users but only ~10 actively using Ariba;

•Want better usability and a tool the whole team will use,

•Concern about document/file limits and legacy data migration (supplier onboarding & bid history); inability to support large engineering drawings or migrate history would block switching.\xa0

(Positioning) Resolution

• Zycus platform (incl eSourcing/Supplier Management) usability/UX significant improvement versus Ariba,

•Zycus also has stronger automation, AI co-pilot functionality & Agents,

•Zycus has strong/mature S/4HANA integration, for faster, lower risk, lower TCO implementation

•Zycus supports Large document handling and legacy data migration capability.

•Zycus is a natively integrated Modular platform, start now where the budget permits, grow and add more functionality later

Mar 27, 2026: talk with Adtian

Discussion Summary:

• You confirmed the decision not to proceed with signing the solution portion at this time, driven by the CIO’s broader SaaS and internal build strategy.

• You reiterated that this is not a reflection on Zycus, and that you still see strong value in our domain expertise.

• You outlined a desire to pivot toward a design-focused engagement to map current processes, maturity, gaps, and AI readiness before reconsidering a solution.

Next Steps:

• Adrian to share a written outline of the requirements and expectations for the design/scoping workshop and include Head of Sourcing and Head of Legal.

• We will review your outline and come back with a proposed approach, scope, and commercials for a design engagement.

• Once aligned, we will schedule a scoping workshop to assess current processes, identify gaps, and discuss AI use cases and readiness.

We appreciate your transparency and would really like to support you in defining the best path forward.




Mar 25, 26: Got a private message from Adrian that there was a structure change and this causes some extra clarification.Legal has proposed the following but still no feedback from Adrian: To ensure we lock this deal within March, we can proceed with a backdated approach by keeping the MSA execution/effective date on or before March 31.

At the same time, we can specify that the SaaS subscription period will commence in the first week of April. This ensures that the contract can be executed in that timeframe, while also protecting the customer from losing any days in the SaaS term, as the subscription will effectively begin upon actual signing (i.e., around the first week of April). lets see if this approach works out.

Mar 23, 2026: Just received this acknowledgment from Adrian: Thank you, Caroline. I’m consulting internally and will provide feedback by EOD.Kind Regards, Adrian

Mar 23, 2026: They proposed to reduce the contract term - we went with a counter proposal of 4 years plus increase as they lose the bundle discount. We also proposed a 3+2. Waiting for feedback.

Mar 17, 202: Adrain responeded to Johns request for an update after the AI counceli call last week

The internal discussion initially focused on AI considerations but expanded to the overall contracting approach.

Proposed change in implementation sequencing:

  • Phase\u202f1: Sourcing and Global Design
  • Phase\u202f2: Contract Management (originally Phase\u202f1)
  • Purpose: address more immediate business needs.

Proposal for a shorter initial contract term of 3 years instead of 5, aiming for a more streamlined commercial setup.

No final decision yet; a follow-up discussion is scheduled later this week.

Request for feedback on potential impacts of the phase adjustment (delivery, resourcing, commercial, timeline).

Interim update provided considering your financial year-end; further clarity will follow after the next internal meeting.

Caroline reached out to Adrian via WhatsApp and email for a call today

Mar 02, 2026: Met with Adrian, still one extra Board to go through - CTO, AI vetting Board. He wants the pricing to go back to the original 500 contratcs with tiering to 1000 more in year 2. Adrian also wants to know if anything is new on Merlin.

John confirmed we can do 6% uplift instead of 7%. Adrian think we can get signatures around week March 16th if the AI vetting board goes smooth.

Feb 20, 2026: contracting ongoing

Feb 16, 2026: Caroline suggested onsite meeting 26/2 when we are in Cape Town

Next steps: Integration meeting with Werner and Nishita

Confirm SOW meeting with Hrishi and Adrian

Confirm with Zoheb Order Form 1

February 13, 2026: Contract discussion ongoing with legal and SOW being drafted.

Jan 20, 2026: Caroline sent an email to Adrian with proposed date for legal call but got an OOO sick leave notice from Adrian.

Jan 19, 2026: REceived redlined agreements from Capitec and shared with Zoheb - email from Adrian - Please find attached agreements with our comments, there’s a couple of items outstanding on our side, but we can clarify this as we go.

I will work with you to find a slot that works for us both to discuss the contract.

Jan 08, 26: They are holding a contract alignment session on Friday next week to pick up the contracting work again and close out the outstanding items. the outcomes and proposed next steps will be shared immediately thereafter, including a few options for a joint call with Zycus Legal.

Dec 12, 2025: We have received an LOI which states we are the chosen vendor, email from project lead: I will try and summarise our standard approval flow below:

  1. Internal Contract Review
  2. Supplier and Capitec Contract Review (multiple rounds)
  3. IT Review (which I believe has been completed as we did this in parallel)
  4. Following 2 above we move into approval according to Delegation of Authority
  5. Contract Signature.

We’re currently in stage 2, having reviewed your comments and sharing it with the relevant stakeholders, this has resulted in more consultation needed - we need to resolve this before issuing you with the draft contract for further review. I will set up a call with our respective Legal dept when we concluded internal consultation. We will pick this up as soon as everyone is back from the December break.

Dear\xa0Florencia, Thank you for taking the time to meet with us today.\xa0

It was a pleasure to connect and discuss the potential collaboration between Zycus and FrieslandCampina.

Meeting Summary:

During our introductory call, we had the opportunity to learn more about FrieslandCampina's current procurement landscape and your interest in exploring new solutions to enhance efficiency and streamline processes. We discussed Zycus’ capabilities, particularly focusing on our composable procurement platform, Merlin AI, and how it can integrate seamlessly with your existing systems, like SAP and Coupa. I look forward to continuing our conversation and exploring how Zycus can support FrieslandCampina in achieving its procurement objectives.\xa0

September Start implementation Phaze 1

14/08/2025: Call with David on a few outstanding items

12/08/2025: IT session

Revised quotations for both lean (ISAS lean) and advanced integration models were requested, accompanied by an updated Excel resource plan detailing IT FTE involvement.

Clarification was sought on API integrations and middleware support, including whether Zycus provides a plug‐and‐play install for SAP ECC and how master data is exchanged.

Questions were raised regarding Zycus' ERP integration expertise with SAP to avoid reinventing standardized processes and to understand potential digital access costs.

The impact of the SaaS model was discussed, specifically the three updates per year and whether these releases would affect ERP integration testing and require additional internal effort.

Next steps were agreed upon to provide updated quotes, resource planning for both integration models, and details on cost implications for additional systems like Pantheon within the week.

02/08/2025: Caroline shared a benefits deck to help David build the business Case for the CFO

01/08/2025: During our discussion, we explored the intricacies of the PPV and NPV calculations and how they align with Forbo's financial reporting needs. David shared insights into the current processes at Forbo and highlighted areas where automation could streamline operations. We also touched upon the potential integration of Zycus solutions to enhance procurement efficiency and cost management.

Next Steps:

• Send Over Documents: David will provide a sanitized version of the PPV and NPV bridge documents for your review.

• Review Configuration Options: Varun and the team will assess industry-standard configurations that align with your requirements and revert with suggestions.

• Schedule Follow-Up Meeting: We aim to reconvene in the next few days to address any questions and refine our proposal further based on your feedback.

SITUATION

Orascom Construction is a large international EPC/construction contractor operating across MENA, Africa, and the US. Their custom procurement system (Compass) has been retired, leaving a critical gap in the sourcing middle — Oracle handles PRs and warehouse/finance but has no mid-process sourcing capability (RFQ creation, bid management, evaluation, awarding). All procurement is currently managed via email and Excel. A new major railway infrastructure project has created an urgent, contractually-mandated requirement for automated end-to-end procurement reporting from PR through to site delivery

IMPACT/PAIN

(1) 100% of RFQs and bid submissions are email-based with no audit trail, no structure, and no digital evaluation capability; (2) Manual procurement reporting is failing a client contractual requirement on the railway project — putting Orascom at legal/contractual risk; (3) Multi-level approval workflows (PM → Director → BU Director → Branch → COO) are unmanaged and undocumented; (4) No visibility over procurement status across complex international projects with multiple logistics milestones (fabrication, shipping, customs, site delivery)

PROPOSED RESOLUTION/STRATEGY

Phase 1 (Urgent): Deploy Zycus eSourcing + Intake Management integrated with Oracle to bridge the PR→RFQ→award→PO gap for the railway project within 3 months. Phase 2: Full S2P deployment including contract management, supplier management, spend analysis. Oracle integration as the backbone throughout



BD: Next step is brief 2nd round of demonstrtaions with broader team\xa0

14th May - Update - draft RFP is nearly completed - expecting the launch sometime in next few weeks - focus only on Sourcing now (S2C later) will review RFP when it lands and qualify in/out as appropriate.\xa0

27th Mar - Clarifications received - they are emphasising local support requirements, raised some clarifications of our own - if local support is a showstopper we will qualify out.

26th Mar - PQQ submitted

17th Mar - Requested an extension to the PQQ - granted, deadline now Thurs 26th Mar.

16th Mar - PQQ received today - with Eid it only gives us 2.5 working days before deadline 2pm Mon 23rd Mar. Appears to be for sourcing only, previously iSupplier, iRisk & CLM were included. Also appears that a UAE based support resource is mandatory. Not confident this will be a deal that meets our min criteria, but we'll respond to the PQQ and review the RFP (if successful) and decide to participate or not.

13th Mar - CPO unresponsive for some time, not clear if project shelved or lost (last update that it was delayed) - moving back to initial interest and out of Q1 - will continue to nudge for confirmation one way or another.

14th Jan - No response to follow ups, reducing confidence level, but will keep trying, may just be the consultant report delayed - worst case should see the CPO at the Dubai event end Jan.

12th Dec - No update yet, moving back to Qualified and Q2-26 until we know more.

13th Nov - Spoke to CPO at the 100CPO event in Dubai, has been some delay while a consultant reviews the internal review (see below) no timelines yet.

26th Oct - Finally received a response to the update requests.... "First and foremost, we would like to apologize for the delay in providing an update on the status of this project, as we have been undertaking an internal review and further evaluating the requirements and proposed solutions. At present, a reputed third-party consultant has been engaged to carry out a comprehensive analysis, given the critical nature of the requirement. Once the report is concluded, we will revert back to you at the earliest." ... believe the consultant is Efficio - will move the close date back to end Q4 until we know more.

9th Oct - Reached out to PC and CPO, Tanya in town, would they like to meet up for a live demo or informal meet over a coffee, no response to several nudges.

10th Sept - Ahmed CPO attended the Dubai World Tour event, got a quick update that they are expecting a consultant report to land soon, on the direction of procurement and will move toward POC demos on modules not yet covered (Contract Mgt, iSupplier etc.) over the coming months with a decision by end of the year ....moved close date back to end Dec.

19th August - SPOC returned from holiday yesterday - most of team still off, some internal stakeholder reviews still underway, expecting a decision in c 4weeks he mentioned some positive comments, but also acknowledged some of our workarounds to meet their (unusual) process, consciously keeping the confidence level low.

23rd July - Have been nudging for a sync - confirmed SPOC Mohamad is on holiday until 9th August.

11th July - requested more info on reporting and screen shots, provided by Anuradha.

3rd July - ARM lead accessed the showpad and downloaded the documents/videos

2nd July - Despite the urgency still not accessed the Show Pad yet, not a great sign - sent reminders

1st July - They haven't accessed the showpad yet, but requested further screenshots by EOB latest, Sent.

30th June - Sent through their requirements (videos, questionnaire, supporting documents) via Showpad (video files are huge)

20th June - Sent through another step, in addition to a Sourcing video covering their specific (and very odd) process (after a live demo and 2 x live POC sessions), also want the same for CLM.

16th June - Re: demo of live proof of concept held on 12th, with mixed success, (Ana had to dial in again due to travel/visa issues) they have a very unusual process they expect us to follow and our fit is probably 70-80%, some gaps we can cover, some we can't/won't - next step is to provide a video of the process so they can review, reducing Confidence level to 10% - if the video is not well received we should consider qualifying out.

2nd June - RE-demo scheduled for 12th June, also sent comprehensive screenshot deck (1st June) to give confidence that we can meet their requirements but were unlucky due to random demo site issues.

29th May - POC held with Dan (onsite) and Anuradha dialling in (couldn't attend due to travel issues)- didn't go well, problems with site availability (also some functionality gaps) didn't give a good impression, but its rescuable - they agreed to a follow up/re-demo session on 12th, following up with Screenshots also to show how it should have worked..... if we have issues again it will be dead, hoping to get Ana to Dubai for a F2F session.

21st May - Formal POC/Conference Room Pilot scheduled for 29th May - DQ & Anuradha will be onsite in Dubai

28/4 Moved to Formal Evaluation, however stated decision timescales makes Q1 very unlikely, moved to Q2

17/4 Demo held (DQ onsite, Deepesh couldn't get a visa so dialled in ... (later found out his travel was denied as he resigned DQ not aware/consulted), seemed to be well received by the 3 decision makers (inc CPO), looking to run a conference room pilot as the next stage with formal decision expected end June/early July

20/3 Responded with new demo date after Eid: Confirmed 17th April at 11am Dan will be onsite - potentially Deepesh also if he can get a visa on time.

24/2: Demo was canceled due to some urgency for CPO - will get in touch for new date

Please let me know a convenient time that works for you. Looking forward to your thoughts. Best regards, Caroline
